segments.title: Sci-fi navies and realistic personnel structures
segments.summary: A Bruships Q&A compares science-fiction navies by how plausible their rank, personnel, squadron, and command structures look on screen.
segments.body: The discussion captures a clear audience prompt from the science-fiction-themed live stream. Dr. Clarke rates Stargate highest, then discusses Battlestar Galactica, Andromeda, Babylon 5, Star Trek, and Star Wars in terms of personnel structure, command logic, and political interference in military chains of command.
segments.question: Which TV or film science-fiction navies have the most realistic personnel structure, and why?
segments.answerShort: Stargate is treated as the strongest answer, with Battlestar Galactica, Andromeda, Babylon 5, Star Trek, and Star Wars compared by rank structure, command coherence, and political interference.

evidence.note: The audience question asks which screen science-fiction navies have realistic personnel structures; the answer ranks Stargate first and compares Battlestar Galactica, Andromeda, Babylon 5, and Star Trek.
The discussion explains Andromeda's surviving High Guard structure and then weighs Star Wars rank and command logic against political interference.
The panel extends the political-command comparison through Star Wars examples and real-world funding or promotion analogies before steering back to sci-fi naval history.
